description_long: This is a wooden baseball bat with a cylindrical shape that tapers from a thick barrel to a narrower handle. The barrel is embedded with several large, sharp metal spikes protruding outward in a radial pattern, giving it a menacing, weaponized appearance. The handle is wrapped in a light-colored grip tape, which appears to be tightly wound and provides a textured surface for secure handling. The wood grain is visible along the length of the bat, and the spikes are stained with a reddish hue, possibly simulating rust or dried blood. There are no visible brand logos, manufacturer markings, or decorative graphics on the barrel or handle. The end of the bat is flat and does not feature a distinct end cap.
description: A wooden baseball bat with a spiked barrel and a light grip-taped handle, featuring visible wood grain and no brand markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spiked barrel head-on and the full length of the grip tape on the handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the radial arrangement of the spikes and the cylindrical taper of the bat.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the alignment of the spikes and the continuous wood grain along the bat.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the protruding spikes and the texture of the grip tape wrapping.
attribute_grip_tape_pattern: The grip tape is light-colored, possibly white or off-white, with a visible spiral wrapping pattern and a slightly rough, textured appearance. No visible designs or logos are present.
